title: API returns 401 Unauthorized after token refresh
body:
## Description

After upgrading to version 2.3.0, we're seeing intermittent 401 errors when making API calls right after a token refresh.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Authenticate and get initial access token
2. Wait for token to expire (or force expiration)
3. Token refresh succeeds (returns new token)
4. Immediately make API call with new token
5. ~30% of the time, get 401 Unauthorized

## Expected Behavior

API calls with freshly refreshed tokens should always succeed.

## Actual Behavior

Intermittent 401 errors. Retrying after 1-2 seconds usually works.

## Environment

- API Version: 2.3.0
- Client SDK: Python 1.5.2
- OS: Ubuntu 22.04
- Python: 3.11

## Error Log

```
2024-01-20 14:32:15 ERROR auth_handler: Token validation failed
2024-01-20 14:32:15 ERROR auth_handler: JWT signature verification failed
Traceback:
  File "auth_handler.py", line 142, in validate_token
    decoded = jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['RS256'])
jwt.exceptions.InvalidSignatureError: Signature verification failed
```

## Impact

This is blocking our production deployment. We have automated jobs that run every hour and they're failing ~30% of the time.

## Additional Context

This started happening after the 2.3.0 upgrade. Rolling back to 2.2.5 fixes the issue but we need the new features in 2.3.0.

title: Request: Add bulk export feature for reports
body:
## Feature Request

We'd love to have a way to export multiple reports at once instead of downloading them one by one.

## Use Case

At the end of each month, we need to download ~50 reports for compliance. Currently this takes about an hour of manual clicking.

## Proposed Solution

1. Add checkboxes to select multiple reports
2. "Export Selected" button that generates a ZIP file
3. Option to schedule recurring bulk exports

## Alternatives Considered

We tried using the API but it's rate-limited and still requires individual calls per report.

## Additional Context

We're an enterprise customer and this would save us significant time each month. Happy to be a beta tester!

title: Typo on billing settings page
body:
## Description

The billing settings page says "Subcription" instead of "Subscription" in the plan summary card. Minor cosmetic issue.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to Settings > Billing
2. Look at the plan summary heading

## Expected Behavior

"Subscription" spelled correctly.

## Additional Context

Not blocking anything, just noticed it.

title: Dashboard latency spikes during peak hours
body:
## Description

The analytics dashboard takes 15-30 seconds to load between 9-11 AM EST when most of our team is active. During off-peak hours it loads in under 2 seconds.

## Impact

Our sales team relies on the dashboard for morning standups. The delays are causing them to skip the data review entirely and make decisions without current numbers.

## Environment

- Plan: Team (50 users)
- Browser: Chrome, Firefox (both affected)
- Region: US-East

## What we've tried

- Clearing browser cache doesn't help
- Different browsers same result
- VPN on/off makes no difference

## Timeline

Started approximately 2 weeks ago. Coincides with when we added 20 new team members.
